I love that this film not only cast two brilliant actors to portray a married couple in crisis, but also chose two people who are actually close in age. (I just checked — Benedict is roughly two years younger than Olivia!) That feels so refreshing. I’m honestly tired of pop culture constantly pushing the idea that it’s normal for men to date or marry women young enough to be their daughters.
have you seen It's Complicated starring Alec Baldwin,Meryl Streep and Steve Martin?
​@BlestservedChilledomg ofc there's exceptions

When the couple dislikes each other, they’re usually going to cast actors of a similar age. The War of The Roses in 1989, Michael Douglas and Kathy Turner were 45 and 36 respectively, which wasn’t terrible for that age range. They’re basically in the same “Baby Boomer” generation. Nine years is a bigger age difference when the male actor is 35, imo. Goldie Hawn, Stockard Channing, or Sally Field might’ve been good choices instead for the Kathleen Turner role and closer to the age of Michael Douglas. You never know who was actually available at the time of production. But she and Michael Douglas had already had success together in Romancing the Stone and Jewel of the Nile a few years earlier.
People need to get over their agism biases. As long as everyone is legal it's really no one's business who is with who

@Selkie7 While I fully agree with you, I think the point that she’s making is that, for as long as they’ve been making movies, older male actors are, far more often than not, paired with female actors younger than them by double digits. The average age difference of real life couples is much less than the average age difference of movie couples in lead roles. Of course, I don’t have real statistics to back that up, but I would be more than willing to bet a significant amount of my net worth that that is a true statement. 😮
